title: Breakwave Dry Bulk Shipping Report 5.9.2023
summary:
Main
in Weekly Shipbuilding Reports 09/05/2023
Stability brings optimism, as Capesize spot rates reach 20,000 - As Capesize rates continue
Source: Breakwave Advisors

Linked asset: 2023-05-09_BreakwaveMay92023Report.pdf
Source asset: reports/hellenic/shipbuilding/pdfs/2023-05-09_BreakwaveMay92023Report.pdf

[Page 1]
BDRY
Dry Bulk Shipping
May 9, 2023
Breakwave Dry Futures Index: 1,703 Baltic Dry Index (spot): 1,558 Short-term Indicators:
↓ 30D: -7.5% ↓ 30D: -0.1% Momentum: Negative
↑ YTD: 65.7% ↑ YTD: 2.8% Sentiment: Neutral
↓ YOY: -47.6% ↓ YOY: -42.7% Fundamentals: Positive
Bi-Weekly Report
• Stability brings optimism, as Capesize spot rates reach 20,000 - As Capesize rates continue to gently move higher,
one can feel an air of optimism amongst market participants. After all, with volatility having abated considerably
from the highs of the last few years, the lack of steep drops in the spot market has translated to expectations for
stronger rates for the rest of the year. On the other hand, the lack of volatility is also the exact reason why the
futures curve has slowly converged to spot, thus flattening the curve, as the "embedded optionality value" has
naturally also declined.
